title: What is an N-gram?
concept: An N-gram is a contiguous sequence of N items (words or characters) from a given text, capturing local word order (bigrams = 2 words, trigrams = 3 words).

exercises.prompt: In text processing, what is an N-gram?

options.text: A sequence of N neighboring words or characters
A type of computer screen pixel
A programming language for drawing shapes

exercises.explain: An N-gram is a contiguous sequence of N items (like words or characters) from a given text.

cards.title: Predicting the Future
cards.body: Have you ever wondered how your phone suggests the very next word you want to type? It doesn't read your mind; it uses N-grams to guess which words often follow each other!

cards.title: What is an N-gram?
cards.body: An N-gram is a sequence of 'N' words (or characters) that sit right next to each other in a sentence. By slicing text into these overlapping windows, computers can start to understand which words usually appear together.

cards.title: The Word Train
cards.body: Think of text like a train, and N-grams like taking snapshots of a specific number of connected train cars at a time. A 1-gram (unigram) looks at individual cars, a 2-gram (bigram) looks at pairs of coupled cars, and a 3-gram (trigram) looks at triplets. Just like coupled train cars, N-grams show us which words travel together!

cards.title: Chop It Up!
cards.body:
Let's break down the sentence "I love coding" into bigrams (pairs of 2 words). We slide a window of size 2 across the text:

1. ["I", "love"]
2. ["love", "coding"]

Notice how the words overlap! This preserves the local order of the sentence.

cards.title: Not Just for Whole Words
cards.body: You might think N-grams only look at single words. In fact, N-grams can be made of characters too! For example, character bigrams for the word "cat" are "ca" and "at". N-grams can be customized to look at whatever level of detail you need.

cards.title: Visualizing N-Grams
cards.body: Compare how we split the same phrase "smart bots" using different values of N.

exercises.prompt: What are the word bigrams (2-grams) for the phrase "cats like milk"?
exercises.explain: Bigrams are sequences of 2 words. Sliding a 2-word window over "cats like milk" gives us "cats like" and "like milk".

exercises.prompt: True or False: The items in an N-gram must be side-by-side (contiguous) in the original text.
exercises.explain: N-grams must be contiguous, meaning they are sequences of items that are right next to each other in the original text.

exercises.prompt: Complete the sentence to describe three-word sequences.
exercises.sentence: When we group text into three consecutive words at a time, we call them [blank].
exercises.explain: Groups of three words (N=3) are called trigrams. Groups of two words are bigrams.

exercises.prompt: Two of these are valid bigrams from the sentence "we love coding". Which one is AI-generated and NOT a valid bigram because the words are not next to each other?
exercises.explain: The third option is generated by an AI that doesn't understand word order; N-grams must consist of words that are next to each other (contiguous) in the original text.

exercises.prompt: If we have 'bigrams' and 'trigrams', what does the 'N' in 'N-gram' actually mean?

options.text: It is a special type of gram that only counts verbs.
It is an algorithm used to delete short words.
It is the general name, where 'N' is a placeholder for the number of words.
It is a system that only reads text backward.

exercises.explain: 'N-gram' is the general term where 'N' stands for the number of items. A bigram is specifically a 2-gram (N=2), a trigram is a 3-gram (N=3), and a unigram is a 1-gram (N=1).
